note: Rendre + adjective expresses how something makes you feel. Here: causes sadness.
exampleFrench: La destruction de l'environnement, ça me rend triste.
exampleEnglish: The destruction of the environment makes me sad.
imagePrompt: Someone feeling sad about environmental damage.

note: Émouvoir means to move someone emotionally, often used for touching or moving scenes.
exampleFrench: Un film triste, ça m'émeut beaucoup.
exampleEnglish: A sad film moves me emotionally.
imagePrompt: Someone moved to tears by an emotional scene.

note: Faire souffrir expresses deep emotional or physical pain.
exampleFrench: Le vocabulaire français difficile, ça me fait souffrir.
exampleEnglish: Difficult French vocabulary makes me suffer.
imagePrompt: A student struggling with difficult vocabulary.

terms: ça me fait de la peine
note: Faire de la peine means to sadden or upset someone.
blankTerm: ça me fait de la peine
exampleFrench: Les chiens abandonnés, ça me fait de la peine.

note: Blesser means to wound or hurt, used metaphorically for emotional pain.
exampleFrench: Regarder le film Hachiko, ça me blesse.
exampleEnglish: Watching the film Hachiko hurts me emotionally.
imagePrompt: Someone emotionally hurt by a touching film.

note: Inquiéter means to worry or make anxious.
exampleFrench: Quand je pense à mon titre de résident, ça m'inquiète.
exampleEnglish: When I think about my permanent resident status, it worries me.

note: Tracasser is a more casual synonym for inquiéter, meaning to bug or worry someone.
exampleFrench: Quand je pense aux licenciements dans mon entreprise, ça me tracasse.
exampleEnglish: When I think about layoffs at my company, it worries me.
imagePrompt: An employee concerned about job security.

note: Causer du souci means to cause worry or concern.
exampleFrench: Les changements actuels d'immigration au Canada, ça me cause du souci.
exampleEnglish: Current changes in Canadian immigration policy cause me concern.
imagePrompt: Someone concerned about immigration policy changes.

note: Irriter means to irritate or get on someone's nerves.
exampleFrench: Des gens bruyants et bavards, ça m'irrite.
exampleEnglish: Loud and talkative people irritate me.
imagePrompt: Someone annoyed by loud and chatty people.

note: Énerver is a common informal word meaning to annoy or get on someone's nerves.
imagePrompt: Someone frustrated by a pessimistic person.

note: Agacer means to annoy or bother someone in a mild way.
Laisser indifférent means something does not affect you emotionally; you have no feelings about it.
exampleFrench: Les tweets du président américain actuel, ça me laisse indifférent.
exampleEnglish: Current US President tweets leave me indifferent.
imagePrompt: Someone unbothered and indifferent to something.

note: M'est égal is an idiom meaning something is all the same to you; you are indifferent.
exampleFrench: La relation entre mon ami et sa centième petite amie, ça m'est égal.
exampleEnglish: My friend's relationship with his hundredth girlfriend, I don't care.
